nodes.label: Stop hook consistency check
nodes.description: brain_stop_check.py — Python script registered as a global Stop hook in ~/.claude/settings.json. Fires every time Claude tries to end a session. Reads /tmp/brain_session_modified.txt (list of files edited this session) and queries Kuzu for nodes with updated_at >= session start time. If files were modified but no graph updates occurred: outputs {continue: false, stopReason: '...'} to block session end, listing the modified files by name and asking Claude to assess if graph update is needed. On the second Stop invocation (BLOCKED_FILE exists), allows unconditionally — trusting Claude's judgment. On success (graph updated or no files modified), cleans up all temp files.

nodes.label: Session start hook
nodes.description: brain_session_start.sh — bash script registered as a global SessionStart hook. Writes the session start timestamp (UTC ISO 8601) to /tmp/brain_session_start.txt and clears leftover state from previous sessions (/tmp/brain_session_modified.txt, /tmp/brain_stop_blocked.txt). Provides the time baseline against which the Stop hook measures graph updates.

nodes.label: Session file tracker
nodes.description: brain_hook.sh (updated) — in addition to injecting a reminder into Claude's context, now appends each modified file path to /tmp/brain_session_modified.txt. This log accumulates all Write|Edit targets across the session and is consumed by the Stop hook at session end. Files are deduplicated at read time in brain_stop_check.py.

nodes.label: Graph enforcement loop
nodes.description: The full enforcement cycle: (1) SessionStart hook clears state and records start time. (2) PostToolUse hook logs every edited file and injects a reminder. (3) Claude works. (4) Claude tries to stop → Stop hook checks: files modified AND no graph update since session start → block with named file list. (5) Claude updates graph or decides changes are minor. (6) Claude tries to stop again → if graph updated: allow and cleanup; if still no graph update: allow anyway (BLOCKED_FILE exists) trusting Claude's second judgment. Generalizes to ALL file types (code, markdown, JSON, config).

nodes.label: One-block escape hatch
nodes.description: Design decision: the Stop hook blocks at most once per session. On the second Stop attempt, BLOCKED_FILE (/tmp/brain_stop_blocked.txt) signals that Claude was already warned. The hook allows unconditionally and cleans up. Rationale: hard enforcement that never releases would make the tool adversarial. One block gives Claude a forced reflection moment; if Claude still decides no update is needed, its judgment is accepted.

rels.evidence: The session start hook writes the timestamp baseline (/tmp/brain_session_start.txt) that brain_stop_check.py uses to query Kuzu for updates since session start. Without it, the Stop hook falls back to 1 hour ago, which may produce false negatives.

brain_hook.sh appends modified file paths to /tmp/brain_session_modified.txt. brain_stop_check.py reads this file to determine (a) whether any files were modified and (b) which files to name in the block message.
The Stop hook is the enforcement step (step 4-6) of the graph enforcement loop — it is the mechanism that closes the loop between file modification and graph update.
The SessionStart hook is step 1 of the enforcement loop — it initializes the session state.
The file tracker (embedded in brain_hook.sh) is step 2 of the enforcement loop — it accumulates the set of modified files throughout the session.
The one-block escape hatch is the termination condition of brain_stop_check.py — it prevents infinite blocking by allowing the session to end on the second Stop invocation regardless of graph state.

The Stop hook provides enforcement; auto_update_hook (brain_hook.sh PostToolUse reminder) provides the in-session nudge. Together they form a two-layer system: soft reminder per edit + hard check at session end. The Stop hook alone without the reminder would produce more frequent blocks, since Claude would have less context about which changes need graph updates.
